success_surface: Complete sheet recreation with multi-criteria lookup formulas, styling, and formatting preservation
effective_behaviors: Explored full spreadsheet structure (dimensions, headers, sheet names) before writing solution code
Identified source and target data regions programmatically through inspection
Used openpyxl for complex styling and formula manipulation requiring Excel-native features
Constructed multi-criteria lookup formulas using INDEX/MATCH with boolean array conditions
Applied styles systematically across defined cell ranges with proper inheritance
Respected explicit task boundaries (answer_position ranges) when iterating rows/columns
Created new workbook instead of modifying source file in-place
Set column widths explicitly after data population for layout consistency
Logged all completed operations with specific details for verification
Applied conditional cell styling (fill colors) alongside formula insertion
evidence: Agent printed sheet names, max row/column, and column headers before solution generation
Iterated rows 2-35 explicitly matching answer_position A2:A35 specification
Applied Arial font, bold/italic headers, thin borders, orange/gray fills consistently
Set ws.grid_lines = False as per instruction requirements
Printed success summary listing formulas added, fills applied, and formatting changes
Created Workbook() and saved to new output path without modifying source file

memory_items.title: Pre-Solution Structural Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Always explore spreadsheet structure (sheets, dimensions, headers) before generating solution code
memory_items.generalization: Use programmatic inspection methods (wb.sheetnames, ws.max_row/max_column, header iteration) to understand data layout and validate task scope before coding any transformations.

memory_items.title: Multi-Criteria Lookup Formula Pattern
memory_items.lesson: For two-column lookup conditions, use INDEX/MATCH with boolean array multiplication inside MATCH function
memory_items.generalization: When needing to match multiple criteria simultaneously, construct formulas like: =IFERROR(INDEX($result_col$,MATCH(1,($lookup_col1$=value1)*($lookup_col2$=value2),0)),'') - this handles Excel-style array logic correctly.

memory_items.title: Systematic Style Application
memory_items.lesson: Apply fonts, fills, and borders by iterating through clearly defined row/column ranges
memory_items.generalization: Define style objects once (Font, PatternFill, Border, Alignment) then apply them via iter_rows() or cell loops over the exact task-specified range to ensure consistency and avoid missing cells.

memory_items.title: Workbook Independence Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Always create a new workbook for modified output rather than altering the source file
memory_items.generalization: Load source with load_workbook(), create fresh Workbook() for output, then save to distinct output_path. This preserves original data integrity regardless of task failure state.

memory_items.title: Excel-Native Visual Feature Handling
memory_items.lesson: Set worksheet-level visual properties (gridlines, column widths) after data and styles are populated
memory_items.generalization: Use ws.grid_lines = True/False for visibility control and ws.column_dimensions[col].width = value for consistent column sizing as final layout steps before saving.

edits.content:
## 6. Task Exploration & Verification
- **Structural Inspection**: Before coding any transformation, programmatically inspect the spreadsheet structure including sheet names (wb.sheetnames), dimensions (ws.max_row, ws.max_column), and column headers. This ensures accurate range targeting.
- **Region Identification**: When multiple data regions exist (source vs. target), explicitly map which columns/rows serve each purpose through header inspection.

## 7. Advanced Formula Construction
- **Multi-Criteria Lookups**: For lookups requiring multiple matching conditions, use INDEX/MATCH with boolean array logic: `=IFERROR(INDEX($result_col$,MATCH(1,($col1$=val1)*($col2$=val2),0)),'')`
- **Array Condition Syntax**: Boolean multiplication `($I:$I=B{row})*($J:$J=C{row})` creates combined TRUE/FALSE arrays that MATCH can process as single condition.

## 8. Systematic Formatting Application
- **Style Objects**: Define Font, PatternFill, Border, and Alignment objects once at the beginning, then reuse across all cells in the relevant range.
- **Range Precision**: Iterate exactly over task-specified boundaries (e.g., rows 2-35 for answer_position A2:A35) to avoid off-by-one errors.
- **Layout Finalization**: Set gridlines, column widths, and other worksheet-level visual properties after populating data and styles to ensure proper display.

## 9. Output Integrity**
- **New File Protocol**: Never modify the source file in-place. Always create a fresh workbook object for output and save to the designated output_path.
- **Completion Logging**: Print a structured summary of all transformations performed (formulas added, styles applied, ranges affected) for verification and debugging.
